purpose: Current-phase task checklist generated from the agent-maintained source coverage matrix and platform capability check. Use it before editing and update the matrix/check after work.
instructions: Start with implementationGroups as the current phase implementation contract: implement each group, then use mustComplete entries as exact row-level checks.

Use planContract as upstream planning ownership evidence; resolve its alignmentFindings by updating plannedPhase/matrix/platform evidence or by documenting sourceOverride/analysisGap when source inspection disagrees.
Keep grouped business behavior discoverable through precise target endpoints; avoid closing unrelated features only through root/shell/whole-file endpoints.
Merge source files only when it improves target clarity and still preserves per-feature evidence, tests, and controllable file size.
Treat mustComplete entries as the current phase's executable row-level task list.
Use behaviorAudit as the current phase behavior audit; it checks closed critical/high rows for source behavior, target behavior, parity, and verification evidence.

Use uiContract entries as static-analysis planning aids, not complete UI specifications: preserve listed source-visible screen structure, route/back behavior, actions, state feedback, and visual evidence needs, then re-read source/resources/navigation for UI behavior the static analysis may have missed.
Do not close the phase while current-phase critical/high entries are open, weakly deferred, or missing evidence.
For every closed critical/high source feature, record sourceBehavior, targetBehavior, parityLevel, targetEndpoints, and verificationEvidence in source-coverage-matrix.json.
A build pass alone is not per-feature verification; cite tests, manual/emulator evidence, or explicit verificationEvidence for behavior closure.
Use implemented-simplified only with concrete simplification reason, preserved-behavior explanation, target endpoints or tests, and source/platform evidence.

If a suitable HarmonyOS/Cangjie API, Kit, example, or package exists for a platform capability, use it or record a platform replacement; do not close the item with local mock data, sample data, in-memory simulation, or another fallback.
Use deferred only for concrete blockers; otherwise implement faithfully or platform-replace with evidence.
Update source-coverage-matrix.json/md and platform-capability-check.json/md before phase completion.
If this checklist is empty but the phase has real scope in plan.md/plan.json, assign plannedPhase values first.

implementationGroups.title: Source behavior: presentation/cart
requiredBehaviors: Cart composes item, coupon, delivery, and bill sections only; it does not render a bitmap preview, gallery viewer, zoom surface, or EXIF-aware image flow.

Cart owns a route-local navigation surface with a close button that calls `navController.navigateUp()`, and it keeps the cart sections inside one scrollable screen instead of exposing drawer or tab navigation.
Cart observes `viewModel.cartState` and immediately recomposes the item list when the snapshot changes, while note text stays local to the cart item section.
Cart updates system-bar colors and composes cart sections, but it does not request runtime permissions, handle permission callbacks, or call protected Android APIs.
Cart performs route-entry setup by collecting the current cart snapshot from `CartViewModel`, applying cart-specific window styling, and rendering the cart sections from that route-local state.
CartViewModel exposes observable cart state from repository flow collection so the cart route can render from the latest snapshot and filter visible items in UI.
CartViewModel collects cart items from the repository only; it does not request permissions, process permission results, or access protected APIs.
CartViewModel performs cart-screen initialization in `init` by starting collection of `userDataRepository.getCartItems()` so the cart snapshot is ready as soon as the screen observes it.
DeliverySection renders delivery labels, a placeholder edit affordance, and ETA text only; it does not show a bitmap preview, gallery image, or zoomable media content.
DeliverySection does not request location or delivery-related permissions; it only renders placeholder text and an edit icon with no action.
ItemSection renders each cart row through `CartItemCard`, which shows veg/non-veg image badges beside the dish name, and also keeps the note editor placeholder visible below the item list.

ItemSection and CartItemCard preserve immediate local UI state updates: the instruction text field updates as the user types, and each item row updates its local quantity immediately on add/remove taps without repository wiring.
ItemSection and CartItemCard do not request runtime permissions; they only render cart rows, local quantity controls, and a local note editor.
CouponBar keeps coupon text in local composable state and updates the input value immediately as the user types while the apply action stays visible but inert.
CouponBar only captures coupon text locally and shows an `APPLY` affordance; it does not request permissions or access protected APIs.
CouponBar does not expose persisted settings, toggles, or configuration options; it only holds local coupon text for the current cart screen.
BillSection calculates and displays totals plus a pay button placeholder; it does not request permissions or use protected APIs.

featureContracts.featureName: Intent, URI, bundle, deeplink, or cross-screen parameter flow.

featureContracts.targetRequiredBehavior: The HarmonyOS app preserves this cross-screen flow by saving the selected restaurant before `goToRestaurantDetail()`, rebuilding the detail route from `DetailStore.loadDetailState()`, wiring the top-bar back action to `navigateBack()`, and routing the cart action to the cart route without introducing URI-style parameters.

featureContracts.featureName: Observable state, ViewModel/Flow/LiveData/recomposition, or UI state propagation.
